feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Implementing National ID with Validity Period in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Hello All,

Greetings of the day! 

Welcome to New SAP Comminity Blog!

Most of the SuccessFactors customers are looking for this requirement - "Implementing National ID with Validity Period". In this blog post, I’m excited to share insights on the latest enhancement delivered in the 1H 2025 release related to the National ID Validity Period in SAP SuccessFactor Employee Central. This enhancement brings more flexibility and control in configuring National ID Types and Validity for different country specific - especially useful for compliance and data governance in HR systems.

New National ID Information with Validity Period - General Availability

You can now configure the validity periods for each national ID record to track lifelong changes to your national ID information. To support tracking lifelong changes to your national ID information, we have added the validity fields Valid From and Valid To for each National ID records in National ID Information.

The National ID with Validity Period feature allows you to have multiple national ID records under one card type or across multiple card types.

  • New validity fields Valid From and Valid To in National ID Information
  • New fields record-id, valid-from, and valid-to to the nationalIdCard HRIS element in Business Configuration UI (BCUI)

Configuration Requirements:

  • To use the National ID with Validity Period feature, you need to add and enable the new HRIS fields record-id, valid-from, and valid-to to the nationalIdCard HRIS element in Business Configuration UI (BCUI).
  • Once the new fields are enabled in BCUI, the existing OData v2 entity PerNationalId becomes unavailable and you need to use the new entity PerNationalIdWithValidityPeriod to access national ID information. If you are currently using PerNationalId for integrations and extensions, please transition to using PerNationalIdWithValidityPeriod.

Enabling Fields in BCUI for National ID with Validity Period

To use the National ID with Validity Period feature, you need to configure valid-from, valid-to, and record-id in BCUI.

Prerequisites:

  • You have the view and edit permission under User Permissions > Employee Data > HR Information > Record ID.

Step – by – Step Configuration Procedure for BCUI Fields:

  1. Go to Manage Business Configuration from Admin Center.
  2. Expand Employee Central > HRIS Elements, and select nationalIdCard.
  3. In the HRIS fields section, click the dropdown of any textbox, and locate record-id.
  4. Select Yes from the Enabled dropdown.
  5. Repeat step 3 and 4 for configuring valid-from and valid-to.
  6. Choose Save.

Configuring National ID Allowlist for Multi-record Relationship

Use the Manage Data tool to configure the multi-record relationship for the specified country/region. You can define how multiple national ID records are validated, for example, multiple records under one card type or sequential records across card types.

Prerequisites Role-Based Permissions:

  • You have enabled the permission under Administrator Permissions > Metadata Framework > Manage Data.

  • View and edit permission under User Permissions > Employee Data > HR Information > Record ID

  • You have enabled the view and edit permissions under User Permissions > Miscellaneous Permissions > Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period.
  • You have enabled the view and edit permissions under User Permissions > Miscellaneous Permissions > Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period.

Context:

If no allowlist configuration is done for a specific national ID card type, multiple records related to this card type will not be allowed.

In the case of changes in gender or other country/region-specific identification information, you need to renew your National ID number. Then, you can configure an allowlist to update multiple national ID records under one national ID card type. There should be no gaps or overlaps in the validity periods of these multiple national ID records.

In some countries/regions, you can have multiple national ID card types whose validity periods must follow a specific sequence based on the legal requirements. In this case, you can configure an allowlist to update sequential national ID records across different national ID card types. There should be no gaps or overlaps in the validity periods of these multiple national ID records.

  • A new Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period MDF object in Manage Data:

Use the object to configure the multi-record relationship for the specified country/region. You can define how multiple National ID records are validated, for example, multiple records under one card type or sequential records across card types. If no allowlist configuration is done for a specific national ID card type, multiple records related to this card type will not be allowed.

  • Validation checks in case of one or multiple National ID records in National ID Information and Import Employee Data (purge type Full Purge and Incremental Upload) page
  • New OData v2 entity PerNationalIdWithValidityPeriod: The new entity replaces the existing PerNationalId entity when record-id, valid-from, and valid-to are enabled in BCUI. It uses personIdExternal and recordId as business keys and includes two new properties validFrom and validTo, allowing you to query and upsert national ID information that contains specific validity periods.

Go to Configure Object Definition:

Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period (NationalIdWithValidityPeriodAllowList) - Parent Object Name

This parent object is used to manage multiple child records of national identification (like CNIC, Passport, etc.) with different validity periods under Multi or Sequential card configurations.

Association to Child Object:

  • You have a One-to-Many Multiplicity.
  • Destination Object: Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period (Child Object)
  • Association Name: toNationalIdWithValidityPeriodAllowListDetails
  • Type: Composite (Association Type)
  • This purpose of this Parent to Child association is to maintain multiple national ID records in the child object.
NameMultiplicityDestination ObjectAssociation Type
toNationalIdWithValidityPeriodAllowListDetails
One To Many
Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period
Composite

Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period (NationalIdWithValidityPeriodAllowListDetails) - Child Object Name

This child object is used to define detailed allowlist settings for National ID validity periods. It inherits effective dating from the parent and includes key fields like country, sequential type, and card types

Purpose of the Parent Object Designed:
  • Configure how various National ID card types (like CNIC, Passport, RP Card, etc.).
  • Define which card types are allowed for an employee, including validity period handling.
  • Support Multi-record (same card type, different periods) and Sequential (different card types over time) structures.

Step – by – Step Configuration Procedure for AllowList Configuration object:

  1. Go to Admin Center > Manage Data.
  2. From the Create New dropdown, select Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period.
  3. Specify the country/region.
  4. Specify the Multiple Records under One Card Type from the Multi-record Relationship and choose Details.
  5. Enter the details in the fields and choose Done.
  6. Choose Save to exit allowlist configuration.
  7. Enter the details in the fields and choose done.
  8. Choose Save.

Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period.

Field

Entry

Country/Region

The form autopopulates with your preconfigured values.

Multi-record Relationship

National ID Card Type

Specify a national ID card type under which multiple national ID records are allowed and validated.

Allowlist Configuration Details Multiple Records under One Card Type from the Multi-record Relationship for National ID with Validity Period.

  1. Go to Admin Center > Manage Data.
  2. From the Create New dropdown, select Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period.
  3. Specify the country/region.
  4. Specify the Sequential Records under One Card Type from the Multi-record Relationship and choose Details.
  5. Enter the details in the fields and choose Done.
  6. Choose Save to exit allowlist configuration.
  7. Enter the details in the fields and choose done.
  8. Choose Save.

Field

Entry

Country/Region

The form autopopulates with your preconfigured values.

Multi-record Relationship

National ID Card Type (First)

Specify the national ID card types (3 at maximum) so that you can have multiple national ID records in a sequence across different card types. The sequence numbers (first, second, and third) and defined card types will help validate your national ID records.

National ID Card Type (Second)

National ID Card Type (Third)

Allowlist Configuration Details Sequential Records under One Card Type from the Multi-record Relationship for National ID with Validity Period.

Multi-Record VS Sequential Record Configurations:

Now understand the Core Concept of National ID with Validity Period - for Multi-Record and Sequential Record Configurations in SAP SuccessFactors for Allowlist configuration Detials

Multi-Records under One Card Type:

In the Multi Record Card Type, we have to mark only one card type as Primary = Yes. Multi Record Card Type is defined with two categorizations. For example, the validity period of CNIC may change over a specific time period, but its number will never change – it remains the same for a lifetime – so it will be Primary = Yes.

For another card type like Passport, it will be Primary = No, because Multi Record Card Type supports only one card type as Primary = Yes.

Now, the primary one is the one whose card number does not change, but its validity period changes – we place such records under Multi in Manage Data → Allowlist Configuration.
Passport will also fall under multi card type by nature.

  • Primary = Yes → the one whose card number does not change (e.g., CNIC)
  • Primary = No → Card number may change (e.g., Passport)
Overlapping Example (Would Be Rejected):

Record

Valid From

Valid To

CNIC-001

01-Jan-2024

31-Dec-2025

CNIC-002

01-Dec-2025

15-Aug-2028

For Multi Record Card Type in one category, just keep in mind:
  • No gaps are allowed in validity periods
  • One record, such as National ID card number, must be Primary
  • Other records, like Passport, do not have to be Primary
  • You can only have one primary, all other records must be Primary = No
  • If more than one record is marked as primary, the system will throw an error
  • Note: National ID Information 1234567893 Please set the same “Is Primary” value for the national ID card type Civil Number in the country/region Oman.

Sequential Records Across Card Types:

This configuration supports different card types in a sequence, such as when an employee transitions between countries or legal statuses — for example, moving from using a Passport to a Resident ID.

Consider a student currently residing in Pakistan who holds a CNIC (Computerized National Identity Card). This CNIC is valid only within Pakistan.

As the student moves to Austria for higher education, their Passport becomes the valid identity document for international travel and identification, particularly for Austria.

Later, upon receiving their Resident Permit (RP) card in Austria, the RP card becomes the valid identification for residency within Europe.

Overlapping Example (Would Be Rejected):

Record

Valid From

Valid To

CNIC-001

01-Jan-2024

31-Dec-2025

CNIC-002

01-Dec-2025

15-Aug-2028

For Sequential Record Card Type in one category, just keep in mind:
  • The identity records transition sequentially across different card types (CNIC → Passport → RP Card).
  • Each of these cards is marked as Primary = Yes even if earlier ones become invalid, because they reflect historical and legal identity validity across time and regions.
  • There should be no gaps between the validity periods of these records.
  • All records in a sequential relationship must be marked as Primary, otherwise the system will throw a validation error.
  • Now in the system, the validity of the previous card will end, and a new card will appear.
    The new card will exist, but the old one will still be marked as Primary = Yes, even if its validity has expired, because its existence will still remain in the system.
  • Note: National ID Information 507080-4870993 Please set the same “Is Primary” value for the national ID card type FRN - Foreign Registration Number, Korea Registration Number, RGN - Resident Registration Number in the country/region Korea, Republic of.

What's Changed

How It Looks Now

National ID with Validity Period

Allowlist Configuration Sample for National ID with Validity Period

How It Looked Before

National ID Information

Technical Details:

Reference Number

ECT-242645

Product 

  • Employee Central

Module 

  • Localization
  • Employee Data
  • Integration and Extension

Feature

  • Country/Region Specifics
  • Business Configuration
  • API

Action 

Info only

Enablement 

Customer configured

Link to Demo

 

Lifecycle 

General Availability

Type

New

Major or Minor

Major

Business Process Variant

  • Hire to Retire

Software Version 

  • 1H 2025

Valid as Of 

2025-05-16

Latest Revision 

2025-04-04

Regards,

Mahnoor Naseem

SAP SuccessFactors Consultant

If you found this blog a useful please consider giving it a Kudos

entry.title: Unable to configure US Benefits in SuccessFactors Sales demo instance.
entry.content:
Hi All

We have already EC Core setup done in the SuccessFactors Sales Demo instance. We are trying to run the upgrade for US Benefits Localizations in the upgrade center.

There are prerequisites for US Benefits Localization which are EC US localization and EC core setup. Since EC core setup was already done, I am not sure why it is showing as pending prerequisites on the upgrade page. I ran the EC Core again and below is the message for the issue.


We got the below error message:

-----------------------------------------------

[Best Practices Configurations Employee Central Core (XX) Begins, The value of Picklist migration is true. Will continue the upgrade with MDF Picklists, -------Preparing for Upgrade------ , Provisioning access granted to the user, ------Performing Upgrade------- , 'Employee Central V2' is activated in Provisioning. Now the upgrade can continue,
BPMsg: There are existing foundation objects: EVENT_REASON. Upgrade cannot continue, Upgrade Failed; All changes will rollback.
For further help with this error, please raise a ticket in the component LOD-SF-SBP.]

----------------------------------------

Please share your experiences and thoughts on the next steps to resolve.

entry.title: Edit SAP Success Factors Timeout Landing Page
entry.content:
When users Timeout they are presented with a page asking them to follow a link to log back in. I would like to edit this page and i cannot find an area in the admin centre where this is possible.

 

Does anybody know where or how to edit the landing page for when a user is timed out of the system?

entry.title: City for country Colombia is not replicated.
entry.content:
Has anyone also had the problem with the replication of the city in Colombia?

The mapping for the address replication is created. The City is also displayed in the interface, but does not moved to P0006-ORT01 in SAP ERP.

Thank you for your responce.

entry.title: Email in SF
entry.content:
Is it possible to set different email addresses, but with the same domain, for each module, from which modules does SF send notification?

For example:

- for RCM: recruitment@example.com

- for ONB: onbarding@example.com

- for EC: employment@example.com

entry.title: Mapping Salary from REC Offer Template to EC Compensation Info
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

Our customer requires to map the Salary from Offer Template to the Compensation Information Pay Components. 

Field in Offer Template: Salary (general string)

Pay Components in Comp Info: BI-weekly or Hourly 

There are two salary types:

  • Bi-weekly - the annualized amount is entered in the Offer Template in the field "Salary" (string)
  • Hourly - the hourly amount will be entered in the Offer Template in the field "Salary" (string)

The idea is to map them in a way that one of these two values, depending on the Employee Subgroup, is mapped to either the Pay Component or Pay Component Group. 

Does anyone know if this is possible to do via Recruit to Hire template or any custom solution?

Thank you in advance. 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Employee Profile  SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting

entry.title: Time sheet and overtime automatic submission and approval daily
entry.content:
Hello all, 

 

I have this issue that salary has to be calculated each 1st of month and overtime is considered. We are not using time tracking. When monday is on 30 lets say and month has 31 days, for overtime that is done on 31 st we need to wait next monday to be approved as this is how it is time sheet process  without time tracking. So the salary that needs to be calculated on1st wont have those OT hours that are done on 30 and on 31 st. I was thinking to delete workflow and only manager to be notified and this to go to automatic approval and submission... is that possible?

entry.title: SF mobile app download and usage monitoring
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Is there a way to report on SF mobile app download and usage?

I know that in manage mobile user, we can export all mobile users, however, is there a way to know who is actively using the mobile application (usage)?

Many thanks!

Best Regards,

Gelo

entry.title: Optimizing Contingent Worker Management in SAP SuccessFactors: Preventing Licensing and Data Pitfall
entry.content:
Introduction

Contingent workers have become a core part of today’s flexible workforce. SAP SuccessFactors offers dedicated functionality to manage contingent workers separately from full-time employees (FTEs). However, incorrect configuration can lead to license mismanagement, reporting inaccuracies, and non-compliance. In this blog, I share a real-world scenario where contingent workers were mistakenly configured as FTEs and the step-by-step process I followed to resolve it.

 

The Problem: Contingent Workers Consuming FTE Licenses

At a client site, contingent workers were mistakenly onboarded using hiring actions designed for FTEs—such as "Hire - New Hire" and "Hire - Replacement." This led to a cascade of issues:

 

  • Consumed FTE licenses unintentionally
  • Were counted incorrectly in organizational reporting
  • Showed active status in the system even after separation

The root cause was a misunderstanding of how contingent worker events differ from FTE actions in SAP SuccessFactors.

 

Business Impact

This configuration error caused ripple effects across multiple business functions:

  • Licensing Cost Overruns: Unnecessary usage of paid FTE licenses
  • Compliance Risks: Active records without valid contracts or dates
  • Data Integrity Issues: Inaccurate workforce analytics and planning reports

 

Resolution Strategy: How We Fixed It Step-by-Step

Here’s the remediation process I followed, based on SAP best practices:

          Step 1: Extract Existing Job Information

  • Navigate to Reporting > All Reports
  • Run and download the Job Info export to identify affected records

     

    Step 2: Prepare Corrected Job History Template

    • Go to Import Employee Data
    • Download the Job Information Template
    • Populate the correct event reasons (e.g., "Start CWK" and proper termination reasons)

  • Ensure all actions are in sequential order

    Step 3: Re-import the Job Information

    • Import the corrected template
    • Use Full Purge to replace faulty job Information entries
    • Validate that contingent workers now reflect correct statuses and do not consume FTE licenses
    • This fix was tested and confirmed in a sandbox before applying to production.

    In line with best practices, this is the recommended approach to resolve the existing contingent worker data and accurately update their termination status.

     

    Best Practice: Hiring a Contingent Worker the Right Way

    To prevent recurrence, we established a proper process aligned with SAP best practices:

    Step 1: Initiate from Contingent Worker Template

    • Navigate to Add Contingent Worker

           Step 2: Fill Personal & Assignment Information

  • Select event reason Start CWK

 

  • Enter Contract End Date in both Assignment Info and Work Order section

    Step 3: Submit

    • Review the details and click Submit
    • The system will now correctly identify this worker as contingent, not consuming FTE license.

 

Key Takeaways

  • Always align event reasons with worker types (FTE vs. CWK)
  • Align with HR, IT, and system integrators to maintain consistent data entry standards.
  • Schedule routine audits to catch anomalies early before they escalate

Conclusion & Call to Action

A seemingly small configuration gap can result in real business cost and risk. Thankfully, SAP SuccessFactors provides the tools needed—what’s critical is following the right methodology. I hope this experience helps your organization avoid these pitfalls and confidently manage contingent workers the right way.

Have you dealt with similar issues in your SuccessFactors setup? Share your experiences or questions in the comments—let’s learn from each other!

 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central  SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

entry.title: Does Event Reasons/Actions supports Manual Document Generation?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I understand that Using Event Reasons/Actions in business rules is not feasible/supported for AUTOMATIC Document Generation as per the below KBA;
https://me.sap.com/notes/0002628604 

But, Is it same for MANUAL Document Generation ? Does Event Reasons/Actions using business rules supports Manual Document Generation?

Thanks in Advance.

 

Regards,

P Shubham

entry.title: Salary Advance (Eligibility for Advances) can we consider all the records in Job information?
entry.content:
In SF-EC,Is there any way for Salary Advance (Eligibility for Advances) we can consider all the records in Job information. Based on the business rule/ eligibility rule its only considering the top record.

Scenario is we need to enable salary advance for people who went on deputation. Its considering only if it a top record. If at all any action is passed on top of the deputation action, then that eligibility is not getting considered.

entry.title: Earned Leave accrual calculation based on Paid Days - Time Off
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,
 
Requirement : Employees are entitled to 30 days of EL, given as monthly frequency 2.5 days. Monthly calculation is based on Paid days in a month which includes working days, holidays and week offs.
 
Calculation Logic : ELs = {(no. of paid days in a month x 2.5 ELs)/total no. of days in a month}
 
Scenario : If in May, the employee has 3 LWPs, 2.25 EL will be credited for that month, although if the employee then regularizes these LWP into a paid leave or missed punch, then in June month, he should get the deducted EL(0.25) from May month. Hence in June, he accrues 2.5+0.25=2.75 EL.
 
Please let me know, in case anyone has encountered a similar requirement.

entry.title: Can successfactors EMLA EC connect to Test Instance of Employee central Payroll?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have an Instance of Employee Central provided as a part of eMLA offering for the project. Can we use it to connect to Test Instance of Employee Central Payroll?

entry.title: Manage My Team Tile Error: "An application error occurred"
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

one manager gets the following error message when he tries to access Manage My Team Tile:

"An application error occurred. Please try again later, or notify support if the issue keeps occurring."



The manager has his own team but he can't manage them through the Manage My Tam Tile

Do you have any ideas what can cause this error message?

Best regards
Lukasz

entry.title: Setting up Custom email templates for SuccessFactors Employee Central Time Sheet
entry.content:
I would like to check if we can set up custom email templates for SuccessFactors Employee Central Time Sheet.

We have different scenarios where we would like to set up custom Email templates but we could not find any tokens that can be used to refer EC Time Sheet data. Like Time Sheet Start Date, Time sheet End Date, Status of the Time Sheet, Time Sheet Duration etc.

Has some one prepared custom email templates with above Time Sheet data included in the template ?

entry.title: employee central core certification preparation
entry.content:
Hi Community,

Can anyone guide me about the certification exam of Employee central core like :-

1. What is the format of the exam ?

2.From where i can get the practice questions ?

3.How do i know that now i am fully prepared for the exam  ?

4.How to clear it in my first attempt and what should my next plan after this certification?

Thanks & Regards

Kartik Chijwani

entry.title: Timesheet Module Behavior on Attendance and Leave Collision
entry.content:
Hello Experts,


I’m seeking your guidance regarding a standard behavior observed in the Timesheet module, specifically related to entry blocking when a collision occurs on a single day.

Issue Summary:
When both attendance and leave type (PL, CL,SL, Out Door Duty) or any absences are recorded on the same day, the system flags a collision error. As a result, it blocks timesheet entries for the remaining days of that week. However, once the collision is resolved, the timesheet updates correctly for the entire week.

We understand that this is standard system behavior.

Request for Suggestions:
Is there a way to prevent the system from blocking the remaining days of the week when such a conflict occurs on just one day?

If anyone has implemented a workaround or alternative solution to handle this scenario more gracefully, we would greatly appreciate your insights or suggestions.

entry.title: Offcycle event batch to "termination details".
entry.content: In SuccessFactors we have offcycle event batch for job information and employment details section. Can't that be added to termination details as well? Because to the standard field "Ok To Rehire" I think it should not always be a manual data change. For example in our client side we have a rule saying that a user cannot be "ok to rehire = NO" for more than 20 years. So they suggested us to make make an automation based on a custom date field where they decide when the employee should be ok to be rehired again. It's obviously going to be a future dated field and there is no rules to make it happen. So off cycle event batch could have been a good option if that was feasible. Currently we might have to do it manually.

entry.title: DRTM - Data Retention Management & Purge for Inactive users
entry.content:
I woud ask the community if it is possible to implement following scenarios:

 1:

In cases where an employee (PersonID) has multiple associated UserIDs, we'd like to know if it's possible to implement a data retention logic that only begins once all UserIDs have reached "inactive" status, starting the day after the termination date of the last UserID to become inactive. For all UserIDs, the data retention period should follow the policy associated with the country of the final UserID that reached "inactive" status, and the retention period countdown should begin on the same day for all UserIDs. (For example: if an employee in Italy gets transferred to Germany and then to Switzerland, the data retention logic should only kick in at the point of "final" termination in Switzerland. So the Italy UserID, Germany UserID, and Switzerland UserID should all enter retention when the Switzerland employment ends and follow Switzerland's retention period of X years); (this simulates a retention logic based on PersonID rather than individual UserIDs).

Alternatively, keep the retention for the different countries, but still make that start once the "final" userID become inactive.

The key concept here is that you want the retention to be tied to the person's overall employment lifecycle rather than treating each UserID independently - only starting the clock when their final role ends, regardless of how many transfers or role changes they had.


Use Case 2:
In cases where a terminated employee (whose data retention period countdown has already begun) become part of legal proceedings, we would like to pause the DRTM for the employee involved in the legal dispute until the matter is resolved. Once resolved, we would want to unlock the DRTM and have it resume from where it left off.

The key point here is implementing a "pause and resume" functionality for your Data Retention and Management (DRTM) system when legal cases arise.
Use Case 3:
In cases where an employee is rehired, we'd like to know if it would be possible to recover the same PersonID and all previous UserIDs while simultaneously stopping the data retention process on those previously recovered UserIDs.

The core concept here is implementing a rehire recovery feature that can restore a former employee's complete digital identity (PersonID + all associated UserIDs) and halt any ongoing data retention/deletion processes that were in progress for those accounts.

 Use case 4

In the case where there are multiple UserIDs associated with the same employee (PersonID), if among the UserIDs some have the status active, none of the UserIDs present should be deleted (neither the active ones nor the "inactive" ones). Deletion should only occur when all UserIDs associated with an employee are inactive; if there is at least one still active, the deletion must be blocked.

entry.title: Limitation on Quantity 1-5 fields on Accrual Calculation Account Type Base Object
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

Can we define custom fields like quantity6, quantity7 in following 2 objects under Configure Object Definition? 

  1. Accrual Calculation Account Type Base
  2. Accrual Calculation Base

As of now, we do have actual quantity, quantity 1 to 5 standard SF fields. These fields are used to generate accruals from recorded timesheet. We are almost using all these fields. We would like to check if we can define custom fields after quantity5 and use those fields to generate accrual from timesheet via Integration Centre. 

Would like to check if SAP SuccessFactors product support custom fields in Accrual Calculation Account Type Base Object. 

Thanks & Regards,
Arijit

Note - We do not have time tracking license.
